Definition: Date and time of the test in UTC.
Date and time that Ookla received the test result from the device.
Local timezone name derived from reverse geocoding (location).
Unique ID of the test.
A globally unique ID for every Speedtest performed.
Public-facing unique ID of the user/device.
ISP name associated with IP Address. In some cases, this may report a secondary name of an ISP, for example ABC Fiber.
ISP common name associated with IP Address. This will be the name displayed in the Speedtest Intelligence portal.
Denotes the platform on which the test was taken: 1 = Android; 2 = iOS; 3 = Web; 4 = MacOS; 5 = Windows

Connection type associated with the test. Most common responses will be 0, 2, and 22 (Android, MacOS, Windows) and 0, 2, and 13 (iOS). Android connection types: 0 = Unknown; 1 = Cell; 2 = Wifi; 3 = Gprs; 4 = Edge; 5 = Umts; 6 = Cdma; 7 = Evdo0; 8 = EvdoA; 9 = OnexRTT; 10 = Hsdpa; 11 = Hspa; 12 = Iden; 13 = Ehrpd; 14 = EvdoB; 15 = Lte; 16 = Hsupa; 17 = Hspap; 18 = Gsm; 19 = TdScdma; 20 = IWLan; 21 = LteCa; 22 = Ethernet; 23 = Bluetooth     iOS connection types: 0 = unknown; 1 = cell, 2 = wifi; 3 = GPRS; 4 = Edge; 5 = WCDMA; 6 = HSDPA; 7 = HSUPA; 8 = CDMA1x; 9 = CDMAEVDORev0; 10 = CDMAEVDORevB; 11 = eHRPD; 12 = LTE; 13 = Ethernet; 14 = Bluetooth; 15 = HRPD; 16 = CDMAEVDORevA; 17 = NRNSA; 18 = NRSA
Latitude of client testing. If GPS isn't used, then this is determined by Maxmind's GeoIP.
Longitude of client testing. If GPS isn't used, then this is determined by Maxmind's GeoIP.
Altitude of the client testing (if available), in meters above the WGS 84 reference ellipsoid. If this location does not have an altitude, 0 is returned.
The accuracy of the location data collected from the device expressed in meters.
Indicates the time elapsed (in ms) from when the user's location information was collected and the test was taken.
The source of information determining location of test. 1 = GPS (direct lat/long), 2 = Maxmind (GeoIP)
Unabbreviated location name; typically the city where the test is taken.
Country from where test is taken.
Country code of country where test is taken.
Region name where test is taken. This is the first-order civil entity in a country level. Depending on the country, these are states, provinces, or territories, etc.
Region name where test is taken. This is the second-order civil entity in a country level. Depending on the country, these are county-level names. Not all countries display this information.
Region name where test is taken. This is the third-order civil entity in a country level. Depending on the country, these are the level under counties. Not all countries display this information.
Postal code for the test location. The postal code is the same as what is used to address mail within the country. Not all countries display this information.
User agent or web browser accessing Speedtest.net.

The communication protocol (TCP or HTTP) used by the test. Whenever possible, the test makes a direct TCP connection with the testing server, which allows for greater testing accuracy, especially at higher speeds. In instances where the device is unable to establish a TCP connection with the test server on the appropriate port, the test falls back to using basic HTTP file transfers to determine the device's bandwidth. For web tests, we provide more granularity and break out the method that was used for each part of the test. In those cases, we provide the method in this order: latency, download, upload. Values may be a combination of: HTTP, TCP, WS (websocket), or XHR
Denotes if the test was conducted over IPv4 (4) or IPv6 (6).

The source of the Speedtest between ST4 (beta.speedtest.net in flash), ST4-JS (beta.speedtest.net in Javascript), or Chrome-Ext (test taken from the Chrome Extension). If the results says "NULL" or is blank, then the test was taken on ST3 (speedtest.net).

User ISP/connection type (estimated). 0 = Unknown, 1 = Dialup, 2 = Cable/DSL, 3 = Corporate and 4 = Cellular, 5 = Satellite. Results with a net_speed_id of 4 are excluded from all Fixed aggregates except for "Mobile Broadband".

This is the IP address from which the request to Ookla's reporting API was generated. The last octet of the user's external IP address is masked for addresses that are not on your network. Full IP addresses for your network can be made available upon request and delivered in a separate supplemental dataset. Devices behind networks with carrier grade NAT may appear to share the same IP address.

Speedtest user's IPV6 address. We mask IP addresses for end users privacy, but show enough to allow clients to see which provider is offering the user's internet service.  Please note that this address may not be the connection that the Speedtest was taken over (unless there is no IPV4 address populated).
The Autonomous System Number (ASN) associated with the client's IP address.

The manufacturer name associated with the MAC address of the WiFi access point (AP) serving the device based on IEEE Registration Authority assignments. This may not point to the marketed manufacturer name of the AP and may be associated with the manufacturer of another component such as the AP's radio.
The frequency the WiFi router is connecting with when test is taken.
The width of the Wifi channel (in MHz).
"TRUE" if the frequency of the wifi router is 5 Ghz when the test is taken. This field is connected to wifi test results only.

TRUE if the chipset supports the 60GHz frequency band.

The connection Wi-Fi standard value:0 = unknown,1 = legacy; Wi-Fi 802.11a/b/g,4 = Wi-Fi 802.11n,5 = Wi-Fi 802.11ac,6 = Wi-Fi 802.11ax,7 = Wi-Fi 802.11ad

The number of packages sent to host server from device. To determine packet loss percentage, take the ploss_recv divided by ploss_sent. 100% means zero packet loss since 100% of the packets were sent and received.

The number of packages received from host server by device. To determine packet loss percentage, take the ploss_recv divided by ploss_sent. 100% means zero packet loss since 100% of the packets were sent and received.
The percentage of packet loss measured.
The number of hops taken on the traceroute from the client to server.

The time taken for the packet to be received after the first hop in the traceroute.
This is the IP address of the second hop of traceroute during a test.
The variance in latency over time which determines the stability of the internet connection.
The result of the multi-server jitter, based on server selection, measured in milliseconds.
The model of the device used for testing.

The count of cores (processors) present in the system. Available for Windows only.

The Absolute Radio Frequency Channel Number (ARFCN) is a unique number given to each radio channel in GSM. The ARFCN can be used to calculate the exact frequency of the radio channel. Within the GSM900 band ARFCN 1 to 124 are used. In the GSM1800 band ARFCN 512 to 885 are used. The UTRA Absolute Radio Frequency Channel Number (UARFCN) is a unique number given to each radio channel within the frequency bands used by the UMTS UTRA. The UARFCN can be used to calculate the carrier frequency. The E-UTRA Absolute Radio Frequency Channel Number (EARFCN). In LTE, the carrier frequency in the uplink and downlink is designated by EARFCN, which ranges between 0-65535. EARFCN uniquely identifies the LTE band and carrier frequency. EARFCN is independent of channel bandwidth.
The type of cell frequency channel, such as ARFCN, UARFCN, EARFCN.
GSM, WCDMA. A 16-bit integer representing a cell's location within a given operator's system. The field will only show data when there is a Mobile Wifi Connection.

New Radio Physical Cell Identity. An integer to identify the physical nr cell the user is connected to. The value is unique to the physical cell antennae rather than a specific cell tower. Valid values are 0 to 1007. The field will only show data when there is a Mobile Wifi Connection.
The device brand as reported by the device. This field will only show the brand of mobile devices and thus only available for Mobile Wifi connections

ASU Level - Arbitrary Strength UnitTypically it is a value between 0 and 97 with with 99 indicating unknownmobile signal strength measured by the android phone forcellular network type—typically GSM/UMTS/LTE.
NR ASU Level = Arbitrary Strength UnitTypically it is a value between 0 and 97 with with 99 indicating unknownmobile signal strength measured by the android phone fornetwork type - NR 5G)
